reason_sample: A 0DTE SPY call with low delta can still lose money even when SPY moves up in your favor due to the potential for time decay and volatility skew, which can cause the option's price to decrease more rapidly than the underlying stock's price increase.

Because a low‑delta SPY call has very little intrinsic sensitivity, its price is dominated by time‑decay and extrinsic premium—so even a favorable move may not generate enough delta‑gain to offset the decay and other Greeks, causing the option to lose money.

A 0DTE call can lose money despite a favorable move if the rapid decay of extrinsic value (theta) outweighs the gains from the underlying price increase, or if implied volatility collapses significantly after your entry.
